basics.name: Charles Ouimet
basics.label: Staff Software Developer
basics.summary: I'm a Staff Software Developer with experience spanning e-commerce, logistics and supply chain, crypto/DeFi, AdTech/growth, CPG, and telecom. AI-assisted development and event-driven architectures really pique my curiosity!
summaryLong: I'm a Staff Software Developer with over 25+ years of experience spanning e-commerce, logistics and supply chain, crypto/DeFi, AdTech/growth, CPG, and telecom. I still love coding, especially when it involves tackling complex problems and collaborating with teams. From decomposing monoliths and designing event-driven architectures to getting into the nitty-gritty of distributed transactions and serverless infrastructure — all amplified by AI-assisted development workflows — I enjoy working through the tough questions, whether it's defining domain boundaries or optimizing architecture.

I'm passionate about crypto/blockchain/DeFi/Web3, domain-driven design, event-driven systems, and patterns like sagas and the transactional outbox. I also geek out on AI-assisted development. While AI tools can crank out CRUD stacks fast, I believe the real value is in knowing when to lean on them and when human judgment makes the difference.

I like to keep things light and fun, but I'm serious about delivering quality. Whether it's diving into code reviews or process improvements, I aim for a balance of high standards and practical solutions.
basics.summaryBuyMeACoffee: I build things like RangeLink (a VS Code extension to stop the copy-paste madness) and my-claude-skills to fix annoyances I run into myself. By day I'm a Staff Software Developer navigating distributed systems, domain boundaries, and the occasional architectural rabbit hole. If something here helped you, or you just want to encourage more questionable side-project decisions — coffee's always appreciated.
basics.summarySocialBanner: Backend architecture, distributed systems, AI-assisted workflows. Occasional side projects.

volunteer.summary: Supporting Cadets during weekly meetings with whatever duties needed attention.
